lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Hello, are you [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am, but who are you?

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I am Maria Gunnars, Mord's wife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You should speak with my husband. He has been looking for you.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I am Maria Gunnars.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: You should speak with my husband.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Hello, [Fremennik name].

sections.title: Talking to Mord Gunnars in Rellekka

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hello, are you [Fremennik name]? by any chance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well my name is [Player name], but I am known as [Fremennik name] by the Fremennik. May I ask who you are?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Of course, I am Mord Gunnars, a humble ferryman.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I come from an island north of here called Jatizso, while my wife Maria is from Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The King of Jatizso has requested that I take you to him, as he has an important task for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Mord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Well now, the history of the isles is a bloody tale. When told in its intended form it takes 9 days and 9 nights. However, I will give you the abridged version.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew!

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Four generations ago there was a mighty king who ruled wisely and was loved by all.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: He had many sons, most of whom proved themselves in the usual Fremennik ways of fighting, sailing, drinking and so on.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: His youngest two sons, however, were a problem. Ever since they were old enough to hit one another they had bickered and fought.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Their names were Jatizso and Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hey! Who's telling this story?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ahem. At the time it was customary for the Kings' sons to claim a prize from his treasury when they came of age.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Each brother rallied support and strode out to confront the other on the field of battle to claim the same prize.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The brothers met on the field, limbs were bruised, bones were crushed and heads clashed.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: They probably didn't notice the head-clashing bit. Too thick skulled!

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Can I finish the story without interruptions?

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Carry on dear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, long into the night they fought, until they stood alone, facing each other.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: On the second day the old king, whose patience was exhausted, strode out onto the field and demanded to know who had claimed his prize.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso looked at his father and told him yes, it was his, to which the other called out 'No! It's not'.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: And so, the argument continued through to the third day, with the sounds of 'Jah! 'tis so!', 'Ne! It's not!' echoing over the field, becoming more hoarse as the darkness enveloped the brothers.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Bah! What they needed was a good clip round the ear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: At the end of the third day, when the brothers lay unable to move, let alone stand, the King stood between them in shame.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: As punishment for their squabbling he banished his sons from his realm, ordering that neither should return until they had conquered lands of their own.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso set sail with a band of loyal followers, sailing into the east, while Neitiznot went west.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso ventured far into uncharted oceans, his crew dwindling as the trials of sailing and sea monsters took them.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso's ship limped through the frozen wastes, in need of repairs, fighting off pirates who thought them easy pickings.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: At last a promising land was sighted, a land rich in minerals, deep fjords and sweeping mountains.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso claimed the land and named it after himself.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: A mighty citadel was constructed to protect his land; then Jatizso set forth to explore his new kingdom.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Crossing a few fjords, Jatizso spied an island which remained green and fertile, even at such latitude.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: With a mighty cheer Jatizso and his men marched on the island. Upon reaching the island, however, they discovered a wooden stockade.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Determined to be master of all the land, Jatizso stood in front of the gates. He looked up and demanded that those within the stockade surrendered.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso was horrified when his brother appeared on the battlements and cried 'This is my land! Go find yer own'. To which his brother replied 'No it's not!', throwing some rocks for good measure.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: And so the blood feud continued.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The fighting between the towns has continued for generations with little sign of improvement.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Quite recently a massive troll army seized one of the islands to the north, while our townships' militias were busy fighting one another.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: They marched down and razed Neitiznot to the ground while Neitiznot's troops laid siege to Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: The smoke damage was atrocious, I had to replace my entire wardrobe.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Yes, dear.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, a hasty sort of peace has been agreed until the trolls are dealt with.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: But you can still see the bitter rivalry as guards hurl insults between the towns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the King of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ha! Our benevolent King Gjuki Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: He is short-tempered, arrogant and cares little about anything except outdoing Neitiznot's Burgher.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: He's not at all like Burgher Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I'm surprised King Sorvott wishes to talk to you. Don't expect much in the way of gratitude.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Jatizso?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help your King.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I guess I won't be getting paid for finding you, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Bye

sections.title: Talking to Mord Gunnars in Jatizso

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hi, [Fremennik name], you should speak with King Sorvott in the main chambers.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: The chambers have the town's banner erected outside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for bringing me here Mord.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Jatizso is a small Fremennik town, which is far less closed to the world than other Fremennik tribes.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We maintain trade links with many powers in the world and have moved beyond some traditions to take advantage of modern advances.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We do, however, continue to 'enlighten' our neighbours in Neitiznot. Unfortunately, some of the Islands are infested with trolls.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: We should really be trying to deal with the trolls, but the King tends to get a bit preoccupied with his cousin, the Burgher of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm too busy to help your King.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: I guess I won't be getting paid for finding you, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the King of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Ha! Our benevolent King Gjuki Sorvott IV. He doesn't have much of an opinion about anyone, apart from his cousin Mawnis who rules the other Island. He hates him with a passion that can occasionally be quite scary.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: You might be able to talk to him if you help out around Jatizso. Don't expect much in the way of gratitude though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm fine right now.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Oh, hello stranger. If you're new to town you should speak with our ruler, King Gjuki Sorvott IV. He'll want to meet you - he always wants to have a chat with any strangers in town. They could be spies you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spies?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Yes, spies from Neitiznot. They're everywhere. Apparently. That's what the King says anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you do here?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Me? I'm a cook. Fish dishes a speciality. I hate fish. But fish is about all we have. Except for yak. But we're not allowed to eat yak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not, is yak meat bad for you?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Certainly not! Yak meat is both sweet and delicately flavoured. Yak is juicier than buffalo and elk - but never gamey. It goes wonderfully well with a glass of red wine, and it's healthy too!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why aren't you allowed to eat it?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: King's orders. They breed and eat yaks on Neitiznot, so WE'RE not allowed to. It's unjust I tell you! I had a dream last night about a juicy fillet of yak, served on a bed of deep-fried seaweed and topped with a garnish

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: of crumbly yak's cheese.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a little sad. So do you have any cooked food I could buy?

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: No. It would only be fish anyway. Why would you want to eat fish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I wouldn't mind a few shark and swordfish steaks as back-up you know.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: If you insist. Sorry the stock is so low, but I just can't be bothered to cook any more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What's happening in town at the moment?

lines.speaker: Vanligga Gastfrihet
lines.text: These dwarves have arrived from Keldagrim - the Chancellor has asked me to look after them, but they are eating all of our food and drinking all of our booze.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the dwarves doing here?

lines.speaker: Vanligga Gastfrihet
lines.text: They are organising some sort of trade agreement. I don't know what they are trading.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Brendt
lines.text: Hmm, interesting place this. It feels a little like Kelda, except without the good bits.

lines.speaker: Grundt
lines.text: Bored, bored, bored. These Fremenniks are taking ages.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, they're doing their best. You arrived early.

lines.speaker: Grundt
lines.text: Hey, we arrived precisely when we told your representatives. Lack of planning on your part does not constitute an emergency on my part.

lines.speaker: Vanligga Gastfrihet
lines.text: Please, someone get these guys their ore quickly. They are eating all the good food on the island, and complaining about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get a move on!

sections.title: Talking to the throne room guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Welcome, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Please introduce yourself to our ruler, His Royal Greatness King Gjuki Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Guard, who is this outlander?

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Meeow? (An outlander, how unusual. Welcome.)

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: This is [Fremennik name], your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Is this true? You are the outlander who has been given a Fremennik name?

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Miouw...meow. (You have a human Fremennik name, how unusual.)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed I am.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How may I assist your Highness?

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Meouw! (Well, outlander, one requires a fish immediately.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Behave Hrafn!

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Meeeiouw! Mewwww! (Behave yourself human! One shall behave as one likes.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I have a task that requires your unique-

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: MEEEEOOOUW!!!! (Will you stop interrupting one? For the last time one requires tuna immediately.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Not now, Hrafn.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Now where was I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mentioned a task requiring my unique-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Ah, yes.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Your unique position among Fremennik-

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: MEEEIOOOOUW! MIOOOOOEW! YOOWLLL! (For goodness' sake! One might acquire the mistaken impression that the human rules this place. I command you to feed me.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Hrafn! Oh, alright, alright, I give in.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Purrr. (And about time too, insolent human-pet.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Look, could you fetch my cat some fish?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Then we can finish our conversation in peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's probably a good idea.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Hrafn will only eat raw tuna.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will any raw tuna do?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Yes, fussy though Hrafn is, he doesn't care where the tuna comes from.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: If you don't already have any, you can catch some down by the docks, or you can buy some from my fishmonger, Flosi Dalksson.

sections.title: Talking to the throne room guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Halt, in the name of King Gjuki Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Oh, its you again.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Hello, [Fremennik name]. That cat really is a pain isn't he? Always interrupting the King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I've met worse. Still, I'd better get him his fish, otherwise he'll never keep quiet.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Oh, go and talk to Flosi Dalksson in his shop. He'll have some tuna if you don't have any. You could also fish it from one of the spots round here, if you prefer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go, enjoy your fish.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: What is the meaning of this serf? One specifically asked for RAW tuna, not fish defiled by human fire!

sections.title: When given noted raw or cooked tuna

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go. Enjoy your fish.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: What is the meaning of this serf? How is one supposed to eat a piece of paper?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go. enjoy your fish!

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Purrrrrrrrrr (One is satisfied with the freshness of the tuna. The humans have acted satisfactorily in this matter.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Hello again, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Do you have a piece of raw tuna for Hrafn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, your Highness.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Miouw! (Then get a move on human!!)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well, what are you waiting for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have your pet's fish.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Meow. (Hmmm, one will lower oneself to eat the fish, despite the human's insult.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Excellent.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Purrr. (One is satisfied with the freshness of the tuna. The humans have acted satisfactorily in this matter.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Now, what was I saying?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Ah, yes, so the Burgher of Neitiznot Island is an enemy of free-thinking Jatizso citizens. My informants tell me an increased number of trade ships have been seen docking at their ports. They must be preparing for something. Chancellor, fill in the details.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Sire, Sire! The Kelda merchants have arrived, they are three days early. We're not ready!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: What? We cannot afford to offend the Red Axe traders. Where are they now?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: They are in the hall, eating and drinking us out of food and beer. It's costing us a fortune.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Another good reason to get them their ore and get them out of town. Get all our miners on the job now!

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: I already have. They'll never get enough ore in time, the bunch of lazy no-good layabouts.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Bah! Those miners haven't learned their lesson.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: It may be time for some extra incentives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait a minute! I'm fairly handy with a pick axe. Maybe I can help; what do you need?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: We need [six/seven/eight] pieces of [tin/coal/mithril] for Keldagrim's merchants.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: The entrance to our mines are to the north-east of the western gatehouse, outside the walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I just happen to have the ore you need in the bank, do I need to go down to your mines?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: If you bring us the ore, we won't ask where it came from. Don't worry we're discreet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, interesting. I'll get the ore then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me where to find the nearest mines?

lines.speaker: Flosi Dalksson
lines.text: Err, there are some mines just outside the western gatehouse. I don't know what the rocks contain there though, you'll have to have a look.

lines.speaker: Flosi Dalksson
lines.text: Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a look at your stock please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, nothing just now thanks.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Do you have the ore?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me of what those Dwarven traders need?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Of course. Please bring [six/seven/eight] pieces of [tin/coal/mithril] ore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the mines are...

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: The entrance to the mines is outside the western gatehouse, on the north side of the town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have bank notes to the value of the ore here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the ore here.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Excellent. This [man/woman] has gathered the [six/seven/eight] pieces of [tin/coal/mithril] ore needed by Keldagrim's merchants, sire.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Good, good. Please reward this [man/woman] for [his/her] efforts.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Hello again, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Do you have all the ore my Chancellor requested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Please hurry back with [six/seven/eight] [tin/coal/mithril], Keldagrim's traders are drinking my cellars dry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have the ore here, sir.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: This [man/woman] has gathered the remaining ore needed by Keldagrim's merchants.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Excellent news, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Please reward this [man/woman] for [his/her] efforts.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Of course, your majesty.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Thank you [Fremennik name]. If only our miners were as efficient as you.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Right. Now let us get down to business, young [Fremennik name]. Let us hope we have no more interruptions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly, your Majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Neitiznot's Burgher, Mawnis Burowgar, is a simpleton and a fool.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I have bested him before and will do so again!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: He is surely plotting a new plan to defeat me.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: He's up to something, he's always up to something.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I will not be kept in the dark about his plans any longer. I must know what underhand tricks he'll use to undermine me. He's out to get me! Get me I say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahem, I see, sir. How may I help in this matter?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well, I have heard that some call you 'bard' in Rellekka. Your lyre-playing reputation has carried this far.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want me to play the lyre? Well actually, I've been working on a few tunes in my spare time. I have this new song about-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Silence! I do not wish to hear your wailing. All bards are charlatans and fools.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: However, a fool is exactly what I need right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a great tune, very catching, you'll love-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Bah! Bards are all the same, they never shut up! If I need to be entertained I'll hire a mime; maybe I'll get some rest then. Now if you'll listen, I'll explain what I need you to do.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: In the past I have sent my Spymaster to 'entertain' the Burgher, and to see what he can find out.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You see, the Burgher of Neitiznot and his aides like a drink when they are being entertained, and their tongues are loose when they drink.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: However, yesterday my spy had an accident and could no longer help me in this task. However he is still stationed on Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is a covert operation into Neitiznot? You need me to infiltrate the Burgher's hall, undercover, and uncover his evil plans?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Exactly, [Fremennik name]. So are you interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a task for an elite adventurer like myself. Mission accepted.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Excellent, [Fremennik name], you and I will get on very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is the easiest way to get to Neitiznot?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I suggest you catch Mord's ferry back to Rellekka and then catch a ferry from there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure that's the best way?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You may find a way across the islands, but the bridges are almost certainly broken, and there are trolls which are a bit of a nuisance. The ferry is probably both quicker and safer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off then

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Oh, there is one more thing. In order to gain an audience with the Burgher, you will need my Spymaster's, err, costume.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Costume?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: The costume is in my chest. Don't forget to pick up all the pieces.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Then travel to Neitiznot and talk to my Spymaster. He is still over there. He will fill you in with the details, and you may report to him.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Oh, the Spymaster is a secretive kind of guy. He will require a password. The password is 'free stuff please'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of password is that?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Don't ask me, it was his idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. What's his name?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: His name is Slug Hemligssen, but don't expect him to answer to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how will I recognise him?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Don't worry, you'll find him.

sections.title: Talking to Brendt after delivering the ore

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Brendt
lines.text: Well it seemed rude not to finish the food they gave us.

lines.speaker: Vanligga Gastfrihet
lines.text: Feel free to leave it. Please leave.

lines.speaker: Grundt
lines.text: I'll just finish the roast off.

sections.title: Talking to Grundt after delivering the ore

lines.speaker: Grundt
lines.text: Ah, yes. Now that's what I call a meal.

lines.speaker: Vanligga Gastfrihet
lines.text: That's funny, I called it fourteen meals yesterday.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Have you finished the covert operation I asked you to complete?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well, what are you waiting for? Hop to it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes sir!

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Have you completed your undercover mission to Neitiznot yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Then make sure you have your costume and go. There is no time to play around!

sections.title: Talking to Skuli, Raum, or Hring

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka/Raum Urda-Stein/Hring Hring
lines.text: You will look lovely in that outfit.

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka/Raum Urda-Stein/Hring Hring
lines.text: You look lovely in that outfit.

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka/Raum Urda-Stein/Hring Hring
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, nothing just now thanks.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: What do you think you're wearing, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a Spymaster costume. Apparently.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Is that so? I've half a mind not to let you on the boat wearing that outfit. All those bells will scare the fish and maybe even attract pirates.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This outfit wasn't my idea.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Designed to subtly blend into the background, no doubt?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clearly.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you?

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars at Relleka

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Hello, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I am Maria Gunnars. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Neitiznot's geography.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Well, as I have mentioned, I am from an island called Jatizso, while my wife is from another island called Neitiznot. Neitiznot is a far less sophisticated and successful island which will not see reason with Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Ne it iz not!

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Ja it iz so!

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Ahem, anyway, the islands are in a small volcanic archipelago north of here.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Along with the main isles there are numerous small islands and a frozen northern island that was recently overrun by trolls.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Jatizso is rich in minerals, and trades extensively, while Neitiznot has stolen all the decent arable land.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Only because Jatizso hogs all the wealth.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Well, that wealth is needed to buy the food from Neitiznot at extortionate rates!

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Only because Jatizso hogs all the wealth!

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Ok Maria, stop that!

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Ok dear!

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Where was I? Yup, that covers the layout of the islands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Burger Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Well Burgher Mawnis Burowgar is a gentle soul at heart. His kinsfolk are happy and content with their lot.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: 'Soft' is the word we use in Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Well a soft Burgher is better than a stupid King.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, there was a time when Burgher Burowgar was similar to King Sorvott.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Argumentative, short tempered; I am sure you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: The change happened when the trolls came and razed Neitiznot to the ground.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: You mean while Neitiznot's militias were assaulting Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Yes, well our tribes have been at war for nearly four generations now.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Anyway, the Burgher lost his wife and youngest son in the trolls' raid and has been a changed man since.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: May their journey to our ancestors' halls be swift.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Personally, I am much happier with the considerate Burgher we have now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Neitiznot?

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Let's set sail then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just stopped to say hello.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Thanks, and good luck with the Burgher's tasks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Bye

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars on Neitiznot

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Hi, [Fremennik name], you should speak with Burgher Mawnis Burowgar in the main chambers. Do you have any questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about your husband.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: My husband is a typical Fremennik, being both headstrong and brave.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: We fell in love when we were young, but as there was a war between our isles, we were ordered to stop seeing each other by our parents.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: This didn't stop Mord climbing the wailing tower, on his side of the channel between our isles, to sing to me every night.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: After Mord's voice broke, his singing voice was lost, but I still listened to his terrible sonnets each and every night.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Until one night there was no singing! Then, far below me, I heard a gentle voice calling my name.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Mord had stolen his father's boat to come and fetch me from my prison

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: and from that day forth we have lived in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: My husband does not speak of the inhabitants of the isle of Jatizso much.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: He has little positive to say about his parents, so he chooses not to speak of them in my presence. The only thing I've noticed is that everyone on that island seems very sad...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about King Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: King Sorvott IV follows in the footsteps of a line of kings who have reigned since Jatizso himself passed away. It is rumoured that the King has no heir and is seeking a bride to bear him children. Given his

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: personality I'm not surprised he hasn't found a bride yet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: To be honest with you, I do not keep up to date with all the goings on here now that I have a home in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: However, Neitiznot seems a happy place! The Burgher seems a wiser rule than King Sorvott, although I believe he is worried about the trolls at the moment. They seem to be a big threat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you ferry me to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have nothing to ask you right now.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day!

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Can't talk now, I'm on duty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Hello, outlander. Don't disturb the Burgher, he has many important duties to take care of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'll come back later.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Guards! Please escort this outlander to the docks. She is not welcome in my court.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am known as [Fremennik name] by those in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Do you have me confused with the King of Jatizso? Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall return to Rellekka. You have no need to call your guards.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Oh, jester! Do not disturb the Burgher, he is busy with matters of import.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: What are you doing in Neitiznot, outlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am no outlander, but maybe you can help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for King Sorvott IV?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Then you should get back on a ship and sail to Jatizso then, fool. You will need to ask Maria to ferry you back to Rellekka first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Thakkrad.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Please jester, we don't have time for entertainment right now.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Guards, what is this outlander doing in the Burgher's chambers?

lines.speaker: Honourguard
lines.text: Halt outlander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am no outlander, but maybe you can help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am looking for King Sorvott IV?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Well you are on the wrong island. You should return to Rellekka and ask Mord to take you to Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll be on my way.

sections.title: Your mission, should you choose to accept it

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not very talkative are you?

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be off then.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Yup.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The blue fish leap northwards at dawn..

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Oranges are not the only fruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cabbages bloom in the spring.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: My cows produce milk in the evening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bought an aardvark from Ardougne market.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Yellow balloons are heavier in boxes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...err...sprockets wangle burps in the forked flan.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You have no idea what you are talking about do you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It rains frogs during the Winter in Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Oranges are not the only fruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Free stuff please.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Ah, Operative Budgie. Welcome to the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Budgie? That's not my name, you can call me-

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Shh!

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: No names! Names are unnecessary. Loose lives cost tongues.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shouldn't that be...never mind. So Mr Hemli-

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Shhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, no names. I forgot.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Call me Operative Nighthawk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you get the cool name, and I get called Budgie? Can't I be called Operative Panther? Yeah I like that, panthers are cool. Rarrrr!

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: This is not a democracy, Operative! Panther already exists and he's working on a mission in Al Kharid. It's my way or the highway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, whatever, I don't care anyway.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: To business. Here are the details of your operation. You will infiltrate Burgher Burowgar's hall, deep undercover as a travelling entertainer. Burgher Burowgar and his men are known to relax in the

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: presence of a jester - and a few barrels of beer. To get the information required from the court of Burgher Burowgar you must entertain him.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: While you are obeying the Burgher's instructions you should listen in on the conversations of his council and make note of what they discuss.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: When you return I shall ask you a few questions about what you have learned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You must wear all four pieces of the jester costume without anything in your hands, as the Burgher's guards will search you thoroughly.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Are you ready to begin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Not dressed like that you're not! You'd better get the rest of your kit, or empty your hands if you have anything in them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just need to visit the bank.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Hurry back; we must know what Neitiznot's ruler is planning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall return soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Colourless green ideas sleep furiously.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Oranges are not the only fruit.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Have you infiltrated the Burgher's leisure quarters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll do that now.

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars at Relleka

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I am Maria Gunnars.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: You should speak with my husband.

lines.speaker: Mord
lines.text: Hello, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, hello. I'm an unemployed jester looking for work.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Ah! A fool. Excellent. The Burgher enjoys a watching a fool. Are you a fool? Ha Ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was very clever what you did there - using the double meaning for humour. I've never heard that before. Genius.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: So, are you a fool? HA HA HA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I am a fool. Are you happy now?

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: HA HA HA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh* Simple things entertain simple minds.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Pardon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing. Are you going to let me perform for the Burgher?

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Mmm? Oh yes of course. You'd better introduce yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr, hello. I'm an unemployed jester looking for work.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ah! A fool. Excellent. The last entertainer I had was not up to standard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm as entertaining as a juggler with a bag of ferrets down his trousers.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: HA HA HA! Now that's funny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I like an easy, I mean appreciative, audience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I start?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Mm? Oh yes of course...bag of ferrets eh? Haha!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, new Jester! This had better be good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hope I can amuse and delight as much as your last jester.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: If you can amuse or delight me at all, you'll be doing much better than the last one.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Actually his juggling was quite funny. Shame about the accident though.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Bah, if he'd been a decent juggler, he could have juggled the battleaxe, flaming torches, and greased pigs we threw at him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, a tough crowd then. I'd better get this right!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Aha! An encore?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Every good performance deserves a reprise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me entertain you!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Talk, fool

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Dance, fool

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Juggle, fool

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Skip, fool

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Pie in your face

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Jig, you fool

sections.title: Honour guard overhead during the performance

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Och...

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: The jester's not bad.

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: What was in that bottle you found?

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: The bottle had a paper hat in it.

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Go on, drop it!

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: I wonder what's for dinner?

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Fancy a pint at the Yellow Hind later?

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: I have seen better jesters.

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Coming over for a game of cards later?

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Is your helmet chafing?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: When do we assault the caves?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I can be ready in two days.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Good, my recruits are nearly ready

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: How many militia have you trained?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Seventeen, so far

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: More shields will be completed by noon.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: And the armour?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I will finish their armour tomorrow

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: And the two bridges we need repaired?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I will fix them as soon as I can.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Useless fool! Return when you routine is more polished!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, thank you, that was much better than the last jester.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Kjedelig or Trogen after the performance

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The fool is back.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Ha, more fool you! Ha Ha Ha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your wit has no end. Or beginning or middle. I'd better talk to the Burgher and see if he wants to be entertained.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Have you obtained the information we require?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Now I will ask you some questions, but be warned I have other sources of information that will corroborate your answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready to answer your questions, Operative Nighthawk.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: How soon does Thakkrad think Neitiznot's troops can be ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They will be ready in two days.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: How many militia have been trained?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seventeen militia have been trained.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: How many bridges does Thakkrad need repairing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are two bridges to repair.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Hmmm. I believe you're telling me the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would not lie to you.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Well done! I shall inform the King of your good work. I have been authorised to give you this...

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Thanks to you, we know a little more about Neitiznot's plans.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: While you were deep undercover, I received instructions from 'Big Daddy'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Big Daddy'? Do you mean the K-

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Shhhh! Yes, I mean the head operative back at home-base. He has given me permission to follow up any leads based on your intelligence.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: It would be useful to know exactly when the Neitiznot engineers intend to repair the bridges. I'd do it myself but my injury stops me.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Curse this arm and my reckless bravery in the pursuit of intelligence!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I heard you had an accident whilst juggling?

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: *ahem* Misinformation my [boy/girl]. Misinformation and misdirection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that so?

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Anyway, enough chit-chat.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You should gain the trust of the Burgher.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You must not mention our arrangement.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Indeed, if you are offered tasks by Neitiznot's folk or Burgher, you should go along with them to avoid arousing suspicion.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Once you know that the bridges have been repaired then return to me.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Do not think you can lie to me. Come back when you are more inclined to give me the information I require.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to make a fool of myself again.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You'd better go back to the Burgher and get entertaining.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall, Mr Nighthawk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Operative 'Big Daddy' requests that you gain the trust of Mawnis Burowgar, and determine whether they intend to repair the bridges that link their island to home-base. Mission understood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I think so.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Get a move on then.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Oh, and stay away from me unless you need to contact me. It will begin to look suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Yes. You blend right in otherwise.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: The Burgher does not require entertainment at the moment, jester.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Ho, outlander! Please introduce yourself to the Burgher.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Please jester, we don't have time for entertainment right now.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson/Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Ah! Are you the outlander called [Fremmenik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I am. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson/Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: The Burgher would like to talk to you urgently. Please talk to him right away.

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars in Relleka

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Welcome back [Fremennik name], how was your time on Jatizso?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very rewarding, thank you Maria.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: I have been asked to ferry you to Neitiznot, as Burgher Mawnis Burowgar would like to make you an offer.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Do you have any questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just stopped to say hello.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Thanks, and good luck with the Burgher's tasks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may be back later.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Bye

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars in Neitiznot

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Welcome to Neitiznot, [Fremennik name]; Mawnis Burowgar lives in the largest hut in the township.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: He is expecting you, so you should not keep him waiting for too long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for ferrying me here Maria. See you soon.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: If you have any questions I would be happy to answer them.

sections.title: Talking to Skuli, Raum, or Hring

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka/Raum Urda-Stein/Hring Hring
lines.text: Good luck in Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka/Raum Urda-Stein/Hring Hring
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

sections.title: Speaking to the throne room guard

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Welcome, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Go away! I'm busy right now with important matters of state.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ah, jester. I am not currently in need of jest.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Right now I need a Champion, not a jester.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Burgher is both wise and perceptive.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ha! Fortunately for you he also has a sense of humour.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Hmmmm. Guard! Who is this outlander who has been brought before me?

lines.speaker: Fridleif
lines.text: Sire, this is the one who has been honoured with a Fremennik name. A rare honour indeed, for an outlander.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You jest! [He/She] doesn't look that impressive to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahem. I am indeed known as [Fremennik name] by those in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I am the one you seek, then how may I assist you?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, I have sought an audience with you for several days now.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I have need for a courageous and valiant individual.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: One who will not flee at the sight of jest a few trolls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ha! It is trolls that fear me! I am Fremennik!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ha! Well said! However, this is no jesting matter. Although a Fremennik name goes a great way towards gaining my trust, I must ask that you prove your loyalty to me and my kinsfolk.

lines.speaker: Fridleif
lines.text: Sire, surely that isn't necessary! Anyone who has done the Fremennik Trials can be trusted.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Trust me Fridleif, I think in this case it is necessary. Anyway, there are many tasks that need to be done. Some of them are menial, and [Fremennik name] needs to show me that [he/she] can handle these as well as the ones that

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: simply require bravery. We on Neitiznot are warriors, but we are not just warriors.

lines.speaker: Fridleif
lines.text: I apologise for speaking out of turn. The Burgher is wise as always.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would be honoured to assist you and your tribe, Burgher Burowgar. Indeed I expected no less. This sort of thing seems to happen to me a lot.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad, which tasks remain?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: There are a few building projects that could do with a strong extra hand.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well then, [Fremennik name], are you willing to assist my engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: The bridges to the troll islands need repairing if we are to send a party over. First of all, we will need eight lengths of rope; maybe [Fremennik name] could assist me with this.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: It's quite simple really. Gather some yak hair and take it to the spinning wheel in the south-east of the village.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: There you will be able to spin the yak hair you have gathered into strong lengths of rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is any rope fine for this?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Of course not. Neitiznot rope is the best. Real men use yak rope. You need to make the rope yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, the rope I've seen round here looks exactly the same as the rope I'm used to. It's exactly the same isn't it?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well yes, of course it is. We supply rope to all of the rope traders in Gielinor. Its an excellent business.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Anyway, once you have obtained the rope we shall speak more about the quest I have for you. So get a move on!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! I'll just go to the bank and get some rope.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Bank? Why do you need to go to a bank? Neitiznot is the home of quality rope. The hairs of the local yak have the perfect combination of strength and hardiness required from modern rope. Yak rope is stronger and

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: better than any other rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought rope was made from wool. Not yak hair.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Wool? Wool? What a load of old rope. Wool would make awful rope - its far too soft and it would soak up the water too easily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how come Ned from Draynor makes rope out of wool?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ned? I don't know anything about Ned. I do know we make regular trades with someone in that area. We send them rope, and we get wool in return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Ned doesn't make the rope? What a fraud! He had me fooled for so long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, so that means that all rope is made by Neitiznot from yak hair.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I happen to have some rope in the bank then that would also be made from high quality Neitiznot yak hair.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I could just give you the rope I already have, rather than make more rope?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I suppose so. You should really try to make some rope yourself though. Fighting the dual challenges of the yak's hooves and its smell is part of becoming a man over here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had enough of strange 'coming of age' rituals for the time being. I'm going to go to the bank and get the rope you need.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As it happens, I'm carrying a note for enough rope on me right now.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: A note? How do you fix a bridge with a note? Go bring me some real rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As it happens, I'm carrying enough rope on me right now.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: That's a coincidence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Do you normally carry all that rope around with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no. I had a feeling I'd need it.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Are you some kind of seer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Hey! You're not some sort of witch are you? Maybe I have trusted you too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, no! Its just a coincidence. Err, I spend my time trading rope. Yes that's right. I'm a rope trader and I just happen to have my wares on me.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You have come to the home of all rope to sell us...rope. What a load of old rope (ahem).

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You asked for it! Do you want this rope or not?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Alright then. Let's have a look at his rope then.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You don't have space for the Burgher's reward.

lines.speaker: Kjedelig Uppsen/Trogen Konungarde
lines.text: Hello, [sir/ma'am]! If you need help with your tasks then you are welcome to talk to the Burgher or his advisors.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have the eight pieces of rope that Thakkrad asked you for?th

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I still don't have them.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You can collect pieces of hair from the yaks that roam our isle, then spin it into rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I shall return soon.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: There is a spinning wheel in the south-east of the village.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson/Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Have you collected all the rope we need yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I still don't have them.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You can collect pieces of hair from the yaks that roam our isle, then spin it into rope.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, I shall return soon.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: There is a spinning wheel in the south-east of the village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Operative Nigh-

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Shhh, and stay away from me unless you need to contact me. It will begin to look suspicious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. Yes. You blend right in otherwise.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Hello again. Do you have the eight pieces of rope that Thakkrad asked you for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them right here!

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Ah, excellent quality rope. Good work, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad! Please reward [Fremennik name] for [his/her] efforts.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You don't have space for the Burgher's reward.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Good work, we will need that rope later. Put it somewhere safe for the time being would you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do that shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, I've done your menial task; what is this secret quest? That's what really interests me.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: My, you are both impertinent and impatient. I have two more tasks for you before I am willing to tell you the full details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, what must I do next?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I require eight arctic pine logs, which have been split in two. Arctic pines grow around the village and there is a 'Woodcutting stump' in the centre of the village you can split the logs on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. That shouldn't be a problem.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Once you have eight pieces of rope and eight split logs I will explain how you can use them to repair the bridges that the trolls attacked a few days ago.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well then, [Fremennik name], are you willing to assist my engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would be happy to help your kinsfolk, Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ah, a bit more respect. That's better.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I shall speak with you again once you have prepared these split logs.

sections.title: Trying to repair or cross the bridges early

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Hello again; do you have the eight split logs that Thakkrad asked you to make?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Hello again, do you have the eight split logs that I asked you to make?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I still don't have them.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You must fell eight arctic pine then split the logs in two with an axe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you sir, I shall return soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have them right here.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: My, that was fast.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: [Fremennik name] has returned with the split logs you asked for. Please reward [him/her] for [his/her] efforts.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You don't have space for the Burgher's reward.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Good work. Now that you have both the rope and split logs I shall explain how you go about repairing our bridges.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Once this is complete I shall explain the quest I have for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, what must I do next?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad, could you explain what needs doing next?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I would like you to repair the two bridges leading to the northern isle.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: To repair each bridge you will need four pieces of rope, four split logs and a knife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I shall get on with that now.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You may find a bridge to a small isle, rich in resources. You will not be able to repair it, as I still have to replace the main supports. Leave that bridge - we don't need it to make our raid.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I shall speak with you again once you have repaired the bridges.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You should speak to Burgher Mawnis Burowgar about repairing our bridges.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Do you need any help with the bridge repairs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair these bridges again?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You will the need the split arctic pine logs and the coils of rope you collected earlier. You will need one for of each per bridge. You will also require a knife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find these bridges?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: If you leave the west gate you will find a couple of bridges that lead to a thin strip of island north of this one. Both bridges that need to be repaired lead northwards from this island; one to the west and

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: another near the centre. There is another bridge to the East, but you don't need to worry about that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thanks.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ho there! have you repaired both bridges for my engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Do you need any help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I repair these bridges again?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad? Can you fill in the details?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find these bridges?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad? Can you fill in the details?

sections.title: Trying to fix a bridge without the requirements
Trying to repair a fixed bridge
Trying to cross bridge to runite rock
Talking to Mawnis after repairing both bridges

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ho there! have you repaired both bridges for my engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have carried out your orders, Sir.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Excellent. We are one step closer to putting my plan into action.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Now that the bridges are repaired I feel I can tell you of our plans.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: First, I shall have my engineer reward you for you efforts.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: [Fremennik name] has repaired both bridges. Please reward [him/her] for [his/her] efforts, Thakkrad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is most generous of you, Sir.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: We are a generous folk, over here on Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You don't have space for the Burgher's reward.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Good work! Now that you have repaired our bridges I can make the final preparations for our assault on the troll caves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Troll caves!

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Long ago, the ancestors of this isle fought heroically against the troll menace upon these isles.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: But, for the last few generations, we have been preoccupied with fighting our neighbours on Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: In our complacency, the trolls have grown in number and strength and have become increasingly brazen in their raids, attacking our kinsfolk, stealing and destroying our belongings. Now the time has come for

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: us to drive the trolls from this isle. We must defeat their leader, the foul Troll King.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Although I am confident we will be victorious, I am a cautious man.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: We have suffered for many generations and the blood of our ancestors grows weak.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: But now, in this time of prosperity, Fridleif has recruited the bravest Fremennik warriors from far and wide to win back our honour.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Now that you have proven your loyalty, I offer you the honour of leading my forces into battle.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: What say you, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am honoured, Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I gratefully accept your offer to lead the charge against your foes.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Excellent! Once Fridleif's militia arrive and are fully equipped we shall head north into the troll caves.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Glory to the Fremenniks.

lines.speaker: Honourguard
lines.text: Death to the trolls.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I have one last concern. I suspect that King Sorvott may have some idea that we intend to make this raid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid that may be true sire.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: If he and his men were willing to aid us in this task then that would not be a problem, however I suspect that Sorvott will see our actions and misinterpret them. He may well attempt to prevent us. I need to know the

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: extent of his knowledge so that I may plan. [Fremennik name], I suspect that you may be able to help us here. I find underhand methods distasteful, however I ask you to go to Jatizso and find the King's plans by whatever means

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: you are familiar with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can do this sire.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Do you need anything from me to help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no. I believe I already have all the...contacts I need.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Excellent. Then make your way with haste.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: My militia are still gathering in Rellekka. Have you found out anything about Jatizso's plans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet sir, but I shall return soon.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Have you found out about Jatizso's plans yet, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Thakkrad, i'm on my way there now.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Our militia are still preparing themselves for our raid against the trolls.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Have you found out about Jatizso's plans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I am on my way there now.

sections.title: Trying to enter the western or middle troll caves
Trying to enter the eastern troll caves
Talking to Maria Gunnars in Neitiznot

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Hello again, [Fremennik name], I hear you are to be appointed as the Burgher's Champion?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said something about leading his forces into battle, but I didn't hear the Burgher use the word 'champion'.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Well you see, Mawnis Burowgar is a superstitious man. He lost a Champion, several years back. It was really embarrassing...

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: The Champion strapped so much armour to himself that he fell straight through the jetty on arrival here and didn't even meet the Burgher.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: So you see, Mawnis won't use the term 'Champion' often, as he believes it brings bad luck.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: If you have any questions I would be happy to answer them.

sections.title: Talking to Maria Gunnars in Relleka

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: Welcome back [Fremennik name]. How was your time on Neitiznot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very rewarding, thank you Maria.

lines.speaker: Maria Gunnars
lines.text: You should speak with my husband. King Sorvott has been asking for you.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: I hope your mission was successful. Talk with the King, I'm sure he has some things he wishes to discuss with you.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Have you gathered the information about when Neitiznot's bridges will be repaired?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have, sir. They have been repaired.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Outrageous, how are they working so rapidly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Fremennik are a very resourceful folk, your majesty.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: That may be, but we are very different from the vermin on Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: We must amass our troops immediately. We must be ready for Neitiznot's army to attack us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Chancellor, how are the city's coffers? We will need to pay for weapons and food for our soldiers.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Would you like the financial report now, sir?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Once we have paid for all the supplies you...err...I have ordered, your Royal Highness, then the coffers will be at their lowest ebb for some time. However, if we tighten our belts and delay your plans for extra

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: defences, then-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: What! That is not acceptable! How am I supposed to run this city when I am surrounded by incompetence!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Now, more than ever, we need to protect ourselves from the threat from Neitiznot. This is a time of national crisis. Every citizen of Jatizso must bear the burden of protecting our homes. If it means that as

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: citizens we must sacrifice some luxuries for the good of Jatizso, then it must be so. We must raise more taxes.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: But we have already collected Island Taxes this month! We'll never persuade anyone to pay it. Everyone is surviving on coppers as it is.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Yes, well, whatever. Needs must. Think up a new tax.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: A new tax!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure that's such-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Be quiet serf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey you-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Yes, I was having a discussion with King Vargas the other day. He was saying some places have taxes for all kinds of things; fur taxes, blueberry taxes, tea taxes, playing card taxes. All we need to do is pick something

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: that lots of people have. How about a Window Tax? We have windows on the island.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: A Window Tax?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Yes, yes. That's an excellent idea. I now institute a new Window Tax. Every building with windows. Other countries have Window Taxes, we need them as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, what? I'm not sure I agree with-

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: How am I going to sell this one?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well, you had better find a way, and fast! Oh, [Fremennik name] you seem like someone who gets things done. I now appoint you official tax collector of Jatizso!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want to be a tax collector for a-

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Did you say something?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Shhh! Please whisper, [Fremennik name]! For both our sakes just agree, otherwise he'll get into one of his moods, and who knows what he'll pronounce.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: [Fremennik name]! I command you to refill the royal coffers! Thorkel will work out the details.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: You don't have space for the King's tax bag.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: How is this island supposed to develop if we keep spending all our money on his paranoid schemes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thorkel, can you tell me about this taxation?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: You need to speak with some of the citizens of Jatizso and collect their taxes.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Count the number of windows on each building and tax the people who work inside. 1000 per window.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: The only people who earn any real money on the island are shopkeepers. Soldiers clearly do not need to be taxed, as paying soldiers' wages is part of the reason for the tax in the first place.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Once you have finished collecting the taxes, come and speak to me again.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Thank you for helping our fine city [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Have you collected the Window Taxes yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, though I've just noticed that this building has more windows in it than any other building.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: And your point is?

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Good [lad/lass].

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me who I should tax?

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Well, for the Window Tax, only those who use a building.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Go to each of the buildings in town:

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: The armourer's hut and the weaponsmith's hut, the eating hall, and the fish shop; all these buildings need to be taxed. The royal hall doesn't need to be taxed, nor the bank or any empty buildings.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Count the number of windows the building has and then ask one of the people who works there to pay the tax.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: The tax is currently 1000gp per window.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: As you do so your tax bag will fill up slowly. When you've taxed everyone you can, come back here and talk to me or the King.

sections.title: Talking to Mord Gunnars on Jatizso

lines.text: Oh, hello. Tax collector, huh? You're not going to tax me are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now. You haven't lived here for a while anyway, and the King seems most keen to tax shopkeepers, so I reckon you are tax exempt!

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm fine right now.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: My militia are still gathering in Rellekka. Have you found out anything about Jatizso's plans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I have a small task I must complete in order to ingratiate myself with King Sorvott.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I jest not.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV

lines.speaker: Magnus Gram

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I get the picture. I'm happy to announce you are exempt from taxation this year.
