lines.text: Well for the Beard Tax, talk to anyone who works in the town; shopkeepers and the like. Don't ask anyone else, as they won't have the money to be taxed! Also don't bother with the bank manager - he has plenty of

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: money, but seems most adept at keeping it.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: So, talk to each of the following and assess whether they are taxable or not.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Once you've talked to everyone, come back here and we will check the tax bag again. Hopefully it will be enough to satisfy the King this time.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I don't care any more.

lines.text: You ask me for tax but your bag is so full of stuff you can't even hold the pittance that is the whole of my belongings! Go away and leave me to beg in peace.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: According to my books, you owe 1000gp!

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But rules are rules. Pay up! You owe the state 1000.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player

lines.text: According to my books, you owe 1000gp!

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: According to my books, you owe 1000gp!

lines.speaker: Skuli Myrka

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid all facial hair counts for tax purposes.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: Surely, facial hair should be taxed on percentage of the face covered. In which case I should owe no more than 20% of the tax that wild-bearded Fremennik men pay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, facial hair is facial hair and is liable for taxation. No arguments.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: That's not fair!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But rules are rules. Pay up! You owe the state 1000.

lines.speaker: Keepa Kettilon
lines.text: My faith in humanity has been sapped again. Thanks for that. Here take the money and get out of my sight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll think about it a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back later.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Errr.

lines.text: Thank the gods, we finally have enough.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Ah, much better. I always find the sound of gold coins being poured into the treasury relaxing.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.text: I have another task for you. Your espionage mission was so successful that I need you to do the same thing again. Except this time I want you to find out more about the bridge repairs they have been doing.

lines.text: Indeed! I have heard rumours that they have been getting some help from somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You'd better find out and quickly. Get on it!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Go to Neitiznot with all haste! The Spymaster will have the details, as last time.

lines.text: Hmmm, I suppose I can trust you, since you have done me a favour in the past. Can you keep a secret?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can recite the epic tale of Beowulf if you prefer. It'll only take four or five hours.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: No, no, no, your old act is fine. I've had more than enough Beowulf. My tutor made me study that tale again and again.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: **Yawwwn**.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Get on with it! The more slapstick, the better.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm happy to humiliate myself for your entertainment, sire.

sections.title: Honour guard overhead during the performance

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: So what do you think of the Burgher's Champion?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: [He/She] seems honourable and courageous to me.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: What about their collaboration with King Sorvott?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Well I trust Mord Gunnars, and he works for the King.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: And the Champion did help repair your bridges.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Yes, well. So are your militia ready?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: I have them anchored near Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Then I shall sail the potions to them tonight.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Then we are ready to launch our raid at dawn?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Absolutely. Long live Burgher Burowgar

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Are you ready to make your report, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I am.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Now I will ask you some questions, but be warned I have other sources of information that can corroborate your answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready to answer your questions.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Where are Neitiznot's militia?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They are in a secluded bay, near Etceteria.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: What will Thakkrad deliver to Neitiznot's militia tonight?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They will be given some potions.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Who has been helping Burgher Mawnis Burowgar?

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Do not think you can lie to me. Come back when you are more inclined to give me the information I require.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have been helping Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Hmmm, that is very interesting. I believe you're telling me the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would not lie to you.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Clearly not. Well, here is your fee.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You don't have space for the King's reward.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Right, [Fremennik name], you had better go and talk to the King. I expect he'll want to discuss your intelligence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need to make a fool of myself again.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Please pay more attention to what the Burgher's advisors say this time. Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Indeed, you look very dapper in your costume.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm. I'm beginning to think that this spy malarkey is not as cool as I thought.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: You're ready to entertain the Burgher. Good luck with you mission, Operative Budgie. Stay alert.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: When you are done, come back and talk to me to give your report. I will make sure the King is informed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am ready.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Not dressed like that you're not! You'd better get the rest of your kit, or empty your hands if you have anything in them.

lines.speaker: Slug Hemligssen
lines.text: Shhh. Go away. I'm not allowed to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, whatever ...

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I have just one final task before you leave [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Guards, secure the doors!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: So you admit that it was you who repaired Neitiznot's bridges?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was following your orders, Sir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was given strict instructions to go along with Mawnis Burowgar's orders while investigating their island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As I told your fool of a Spymaster, Neitiznot's militia are not going to attack you.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Then why does Burgher Mawnis Burowgar amass an army?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is no army Neitiznot has trained, but a raiding party, sire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mawnis Burowgar's goal is to see the leader of the trolls beheaded.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Do you take me for a fool [Fremennik name]? It's all lies!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You must have been brainwashed by my cousin! He is EVIL and wants to destroy me! Jah! Tiz so! Jah! Tiz so!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right! That's it! I've had enough!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No more lying! No more biting my tongue! Do I take you for a fool? Yes I do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are so paranoid about Neitiznot that you are running this island into the ground!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Neitiznot is not going to attack Jatizso. I'll say it again to try and force it into your thick skull! Neitiznot is not going to attack Jatizso. They don't care about Jatizso. They care about the trolls and Troll King who

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: are the real threats to the islands. You are so obsessed with your cousin, however, that you stick your head

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: in the sand whenever it comes to the trolls. But I'm not sure it matters, since I think there is a good chance that you will destroy this village long before the trolls get here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do not have to dress in a jester's costume to act the fool, your royal crown is more than enough!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: No-one has ever talked to me like that before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe if they had, you wouldn't be in this mess.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: I should have you beheaded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to see you try.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: So it seems that my cousin's plan is to demonstrate his superiority by killing the Troll King. I cannot let that happen! I will not let him steal the glory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You haven't listened to a single word I've been saying, have you?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: We amass our forces now! There is no time to lose.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: At dawn my men will march upon my ancestor's nemesis, the Troll King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you even know where to find the Troll King? Your warriors are underfed and tired; your weapons inadequate. You don't stand a chance. Join with your brethren and you may share the glory and perhaps find

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: a way to make peace between your lands. I'm sure the Burgher would be glad of your aid.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Bah! Don't be ridiculous! He would just stab me in the back and claim the glory himself. My brave warriors will find the Troll King and slay him first. How could they not, they are Jatizso warriors, not Neitiznot scum.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You will deliver my decree to Burgher Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You shall inform him that my troops attack the western troll caves at dawn and that his interference shall be considered an act of WAR! Long live Jatizso! Long live ME!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. I give up. I will do this final task for you. After that you're on your own - may the gods be with your troops - they're going to need it.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Fine. Take the decree and go.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Have you delivered the decree yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well, get a move-on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I've lost the decree.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: You've lost it! Bah! It's a good thing I'm used to working with incompetents!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have the space to carry it.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: More incompetence! Well, come back when you have.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Well? Get moving!

lines.speaker: Trogen Konungarde/Kjedelig Uppsen
lines.text: Ah [Fremennik name], the Burgher is keen to see you. Walk on through.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You should speak to Fridleif or the Burgher about crafting yourself the traditional armour of a Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Thakkrad.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: My militia are en route from Rellekka. Have you found out anything about Jatizso's plans yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was instructed to give you this, err, now where did I put the King's decree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have, sir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was instructed to give you this decree by King Sorvott.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Sorvott has spies that have learnt of the troops you have trained.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Until recently, they believed that you planned to assault their town.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: The paranoid fool.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: It would bring great shame upon my family to break the truce that was signed between King Sorvott III and my father.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now that Jatizso's spies have learnt of your plans to attack the troll caves, King Sorvott is raising his guards to assault them himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe he plans to depart before you can be ready.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, if he thinks the folk of Neitiznot can be intimidated by his threats, he best be ready to accept defeat.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Blasted spies, how can King Sorvott call himself a Fremennik when he resorts to such dishonourable tactics?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: We have no time to lose. Fridleif, please guide [Fremennik name] through the traditions laid down by our ancestors.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Once you have completed these rites I shall appoint you as head of my raiding party.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Indeed, it is written in the sagas of Neitiznot that the Champion of the isle must equip himself with the tools of a Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I offer to act as guide to [Fremennik name] during the traditional rites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. What do these rites entail?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Firstly, you must equip yourself with the traditional armour of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You will need to cull several yaks in order to craft yourself a suit of yak-hide armour.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Once you have a few pieces of yak-hide, return to me and I shall cure them for you.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Then I will give you instruction on how this hide may be crafted into a thick jerkin, ideal for protecting yourself from the granite clubs that the trolls of these isles prefer to wield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Fridleif. I shall return with the yak-hides soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have still to complete the traditional armour of a Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad? Explain to [Fremennik name] what she needs to do again.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Of course, sire.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You will need to cull several yaks in order to craft yourself a suit of yak-hide armour. There is a fenced-in field to the west of the village where yaks roam freely. Once you have a few pieces of yak-hide, return to me

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Thakkrad.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: To make the traditional armour of a Neitiznot warrior you will need some yak-hide to make the armour.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Once you have the yak-hide, Thakkrad can cure it for you.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: To make the shield you will need some arctic pine, rope and bronze nails. Then you will need to find the 'Woodcutting stumps' to make make the shield on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the advice, Fridlief. Where are these 'Woodcutting stumps'?

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You will find the Woodcutting stumps near the town's 'geyser'.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Why are you talking to me? Surely you should be hanging out with your Neitiznot friends now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright I will - if that's the way you're going to be about it.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

sections.title: Talking with Thakkrad or Maris with yak-hide

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson/Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You are carrying yak-hide, are you not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson/Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: The aroma, outlander! It's very distinctive. So, do you wish to get your hide cured?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cure my yak-hide, please.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: I will cure yak-hide for a fee of 5 gp per hide.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You don't have enough gold to pay me!

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you cure any other type of leather?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Other types of leather? Why would you need any other type of leather?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take that as a no then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thanks. I'll be off then.

sections.title: Talking to Mawnis after having made one piece of yak-hide armour

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I see you have made some of the armour. Do you need assistance with the other part?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please, I've forgotten how to make the armour.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad, can you explain?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You will need to cull several yaks in order to craft yourself a suit of yak-hide armour. There is a fenced-in field to the west of the village where yaks roam freely. Once you have a few pieces of yak-hide, return to me

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: and I shall cure them for you. Once cured, a normal needle and thread will be needed to craft the yak leather in the same way as any other leather.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Thakkrad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thanks. I'll be off then.

sections.title: Talking to Mawnis after making a full set of yak-hide

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Have you completed your suit of yak armour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thakkrad has helped me to complete the traditional armour of a Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Excellent news. Now you have just one more item to craft and I can appoint you as leader of my warband; then you may lead our raid against the trolls.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: The final piece of traditional arms is a large Fremennik round shield.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: With this you will be able to deflect the large rocks that some of the trolls like to throw at you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How may this be crafted?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: You will need two arctic pine logs, a length of rope, some bronze nails and a hammer.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: When you have all of these you should find the tree stump outside of my hut. You will be able to hammer together the shield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. I shall return with a shield soon.

sections.title: Talking to Mawnis before making the shield

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Have you completed the final piece of your Fremennik garb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have still to complete the traditional shield of a Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Have you completed the final piece of your Fremennik garb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fridleif has helped me to complete the traditional shield of the Fremennik warrior.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Excellent work, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: There is little more for me to say. You have proven your worth to my kinsmen and I, but I must continue with the traditions so...

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Do you, [Fremennik name], solemnly swear to uphold the virtues of courage and honour?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Thakkrad whispers in your ear. Just repeat what I say.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: 'I, [Fremennik name], swear to uphold the virtues of courage and honour.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I [Fremennik name] swear to uphold the virtues of courage and honour.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Then it is done.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I hearby appoint [Fremennik name] as Champion of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Praise be; may the blood of our ancestors flow strongly in our new Champion.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Welcome to the clan of Neitiznot; you shall be welcome in this township forever more.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: You have made our township very proud today. We hope you continue to match up to our hopes in the quest which we have for you.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: As my kinsmen have mentioned, there is a foul beast dwelling in the caves on the northernmost isle.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: We ask that you lead our militia against this brute and return with the beast's head as proof of your heroism.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: My guards have secured a foothold within the easternmost caves.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: We have not seen Jatizso's militia yet, but some of my scouts have reported hearing distant fighting in the tunnels.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I shall make my way over to them now, so that we may defeat this foul Troll King.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You should head to the eastern end of the northern isle. You don't want to run in to Jatizso's troops now.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Fridleif's scouts have reported that the militia have entered the caves to the east of the northern isle. They are waiting for you to lead them in battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Thakkrad, i'm on my way there now.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Our militia have entered the caves to the east of the northern isle.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You should hurry to meet them before they are discovered by the trolls. Make sure you go to the right cave entrance, otherwise you won't find them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, the eastern caves on the northern isle. Thanks Fridleif, I'm on my way there now.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the western or central caves

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Left, right, then left again... I'm lost. Best I head toward the sound of the fighting.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Hold your ground men!

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: The Champion had better arrive soon. We can't wait much longer!

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: [He/She] will be here soon. The Burgher trusts [Him/Her]. That's good enough for me.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Sir, Champion [Fremennik name] has arrived! I knew [He/She] wouldn't let us down!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a hard journey here. I trust you all made it here intact?

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: A little bruised but nothing a true Fremennik can't handle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well said!

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Our supplies are starting to run a little low; we should make our charge soon.

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Once you have helped to slay 10 trolls then you should be able to assault the Troll King.

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: We will hold back the troll forces while you cross the bridge to the south of the cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will find the Troll King there, correct?

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Yes madam! I have some supplies with me if you need them, but I only have a small amount left. We took a heavy toll getting here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The fact you've saved any at all proves your bravery.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Thank you madam. We have food, Strength potions and Prayer potions to see us through this battle.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: A fine tale we will tell once you have claimed the beast's head.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Men, follow [Fremennik name]'s orders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready, men?

lines.speaker: Honour guard
lines.text: Aye Champion!.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Aye [sir/madam]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Warriors, advance!

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Charge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bork! The battle rages on and we are strong, but I need supplies!

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: What do you need, brave [Fremennik name], madam?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me food, Bork!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me food, Bork!

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Here, take this tuna quickly, madam!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need Strength potions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need Strength potions.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: Strength be with you, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need Prayer potions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need Prayer potions.

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: May the gods be with you [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't need anything.

sections.title: Talking to Bork after claiming all supplies

lines.speaker: Bork Sigmundson
lines.text: I've run out of supplies, [Fremennik name]! Only our bravery remains.

sections.title: Talking to King Sorvott after the start of the battle

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: My guards will beat you to your goal yet.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: The weakness of wood and leather will be bested by my steel and mithril; mark my words.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I sense defeat in your voice already, mighty King.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You and I both know that Burgher Burowgar's forces are better prepared and trained than your men.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Guards, prepare my gallows at once.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: I would be leaving now if I were you, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're right Thorkel, goodbye.

sections.title: Trying to cross the bridge before killing 10 trolls

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that!

lines.speaker: Ice Troll King
lines.text: Gruurg!

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: What are you doing in my court? Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to inform you that you may recall your guards; the Troll King was slain by my hand.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Preposterous. Lies, I tell you, lies!

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Yes, your majesty, of course your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guards! Where are your Guards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Are they still recovering from their defeat against the trolls?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Outlander, leave me in peace before I am forced to bear arms against you myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, your Highness, I leave you to contemplate this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should you have approached me honestly and truthfully, rather than with lies and secrecy,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: then it could have been you holding the head of the Troll King aloft today.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Be gone with you. I have heard enough of your insults for one day.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Thanks, [Fremennik name], you are the first person to stand up to the King's bullying in many a year. Maybe he has learnt something today?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We can but hope Thorkel.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Fare thee well brave warrior, fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Hrafn
lines.text: Purrrrz! (One wishes the tuna-bearing outlander well. One would prefer for [him/her] to stay.)

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Useless, disloyal cat.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Hail the conquering hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh. Thanks - I was pretty heroic even if I say so myself.

lines.speaker: Mord Gunnars
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson/Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Congratulations on defeating the Troll King. I hope you have collected the fine rewards that the Burgher Mawnis Burowgar has for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have returned victorious, Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Do you bring proof of your victory?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: You should bring proof to present to the Burgher.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have returned victorious, Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Hail, Champion of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I offer the beast's head as proof that I have completed your task, sir.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I thank you for bringing me this trophy, [Fremennik name], you have done much to bring honour to this isle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My thanks for the honour you have bestowed upon me, Mawnis Burowgar.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: As a mark of my gratitude for slaying my nemesis, the foul Troll King, I offer you my helm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thank you for presenting me with such a finely crafted helm, sir.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: It makes me proud to know that the helm of my ancestors will be worn in battle.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I thank you on behalf of all my Kinsmen, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Praise be to our brave Champion.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Hail, Champion [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Thakkrad, I believe you have finished repairing the remaining bridge.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: That I have sir, we should be able to mine 'runite' once again; now that I have repaired the north-eastern bridge.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ah, so that is how we compete with the mineral wealth of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Indeed, the wealth of these isles may not be spread evenly, but we certainly make the most of what we have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I look forward to any other tasks you may have for me in the future, Burgher Burowar.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: My next task is to make peace with the King of Jatizso.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do not think I can help you with that sire. I don't think King Sorvott is speaking with me anymore.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Ah, I see you too have angered King Sorvott IV.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Not to worry, I have my ways of pacifying the King.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Fare thee well, my Champion.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Fare thee well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair thee well, kinsmen of Neitiznot.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I am proud that the helm of my ancestors will be worn in battle. I thank you on behalf of all my kinsmen, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thank you again for presenting me with such a helm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any more of those helms?

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: What, the priceless heirloom that I gave to you as a sign of my trust and gratitude?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err yes, that one. I may have mislaid it.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: It's a good job I have alert and loyal men who notice when such a precious item is left laying around, and who pick it up.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: I'm afraid I'm going to have to charge you a 50,000 coin handling cost.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, yes, I do. But I'm going to have to charge you 50,000 coins - these are precious, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much money on me.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, I accept.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I can afford that, sorry.

lines.speaker: Mawnis Burowgar
lines.text: Well, come back when you can.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: What are you doing in my court? Guards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come to inform you that you may recall your guards; the Troll King was slain by my hand.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Preposterous. Lies, I tell you, lies!

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Yes, your majesty, of course your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Guards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guards! Where are your Guards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Are they still recovering from their defeat against the trolls?

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Outlander, leave me in peace before I am forced to bear arms against you myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well, your Highness, I leave you to contemplate this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should you have approached me honestly and truthfully, rather than with lies and secrecy,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: then it could have been you holding the head of the Troll King aloft today.

lines.speaker: King Sorvott IV
lines.text: Be gone with you. I have heard enough of your insults for one day.

lines.speaker: Thorkel Silkbeard
lines.text: Thanks, [Fremennik name], you are the first person to stand up to the King's bullying in many a year. Maybe he has learnt something today?

lines.speaker: Trogen Konungarde/Kjedelig Uppsen
lines.text: Ho, [Fremennik name]! The brave warrior returns!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a hard-fought battle, but we Fremennik prevailed!

lines.speaker: Trogen Konungarde/Kjedelig Uppsen
lines.text: I expect we'll be hearing songs about the 'Slayer of the Troll King' for many years to come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It would be an honour to be remembered in such a way! Thanks.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Thank you for leading the Burgher's militia against the Troll King.

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Now that the trolls are leaderless I have repaired the bridge to the central isle for you as best I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Thakkrad. Does that mean I have access to the runite ores on that island?

lines.speaker: Thakkrad Sigmundson
lines.text: Yes, you should be able to mine runite there if you wish.

lines.speaker: Fridleif Shieldson
lines.text: Congratulations, Champion [Fremennik name]. Thank you for defeating the Troll King.
